content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcal PRACTTcBs C oMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 . 428 J Street o Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
February 8,2002
Larry Walker Auditor Committee
Post Office Box 968
Chino, CA 91708-0968
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-01-302
Dear Mr. Walker:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").I
QUESTTON
How should two candidates report receipts from a joint fundraiser?
CONCLUSION
See analysis below.
FACTS
The Lary Walker (Walker) and Herb Fischer (Fischer) committees held a joint
fundraising reception in November. Contributions were made payable to Fischer, as
directed by the invitation. Expenses were paid Yz eachby the two committees, with each
committee writing a check for lz directly to the vendor. The committees agreed to divide
the proceeds of the event 50Yo each. Fischer now has (approximately) $:al,OOO, and will
write a Fischer Committee check for $17,000 to Walker to accomplish the agreed-upon
division.
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
I 8 I 0 9- 1 8 997, of the California Code of Regulations.
ffi


File No. A-01-302
Page No. 2
ANALYSIS
Candidate Fischer's Report:
Schedule A: Mr. Fischer must report $17,000 as contributions received on
Schedule A, rtemized as to each proportional amount to be allocated to that campaign.
For example, a $1,000 check fromXYZ,Inc. is to be reported as a $500 contribution
received by Fischer. The resulting Schedule A would show a total of $ 17,000 in
contributions received.
Schedule I: To account for the other $17,000 Fischer received (and which will be
forwarded to Walker), Fischer should show a positive adjustment to cash of $17,000,
with the explanation that this amount was received on Walker's behalf, and has been
forwarded to Walker.
Walker Report
Schedule A: Mr. Walker should report the $17,000 received from Fischer by
listing essentially the same information that Fischer listed on Schedule A. For example,
the XYZ,Inc. check will be shown as a $500 contribution here also. Fischer will be cited
as an intermediary for the entire $17,000.
A small number of checks were also received by Walker (all under $100, and
totaling under $1,000). You should handle these in the same way as above, with roles
reversed, and note that no additional itemization of contributors will result, but an
additional amount for contributions under $100 will result on both reports. Also, a much
smaller adjustment to cash will appear on the Walker report.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660
Sincerely,
Luisa
By:
Scott Tocher
el,Legal Division
